name: Egg Rolls B1
recipeIngredient: 1/3 cup Finely Chopped Green Onions (with tops)
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the mushrooms in warm water until soft, approximately 30 minutes; drain.
2. Rinse in warm water; drain. Remove and discard stems. Cut caps into thin strips. Set aside.
3. Mix pork, 1/2 tsp of salt, cornstarch, soy sauce, and pepper. Cover and refrigerate for about 20 minutes.
4. Heat 2 quarts of water to boiling in a Dutch oven. Add the cabbage. Heat again to boiling; cover and cook for 1 minute. Drain thoroughly and remove excess water by squeezing the cabbage.
5. Heat a wok until 2 drops of water bubble and skitter when sprinkled into the wok. Add 2 tablespoons of oil and rotate to coat sides.
6. Add seasoned pork; stir-fry until no longer pink. Add mushrooms and bamboo shoots; stir-fry for 1 minute.
7. Stir in cabbage, shrimp, green onions, remaining 1 tsp salt, and five spice powder. Cool.
8. Place about 1/2 cup of egg roll filling slightly below the center of an egg roll skin. Cover the remaining skins with a dampened towel to keep them pliable.
9. Fold the corner of the skin closest to the filling over, tucking the point under the filling. Fold in and overlap the two opposite corners. Brush the fourth corner with egg and roll up to seal. Repeat with remaining skins.
10. Cover filled rolls with a dampened towel or plastic wrap to prevent drying out.
11. Pour remaining oil into a wok to a depth of 2 inches and heat to 350°F. Fry four or five rolls at a time for 2-3 minutes until golden brown, turning three times. Drain on paper towels and keep hot.
12. Meanwhile, stir mustard with 3 tablespoons plus 1/2 teaspoon cold water until smooth. Let stand for 5 minutes before serving.
13. Serve egg rolls with the hot mustard and Red Sweet & Sour Sauce. The sauce can be mixed in a small bowl and served immediately or covered and refrigerated.
